answer:
In Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation*, Volume I, Chapter 5, men are subject by their physical organization—sharing the same organs, faculties, needs, and capacity for habits—to invariable *natural laws* that dictate how they prosper or decline.[^1] These laws are constant relations between causes (from the physical world and human actions) and their effects, producing identical outcomes under similar conditions.[^2]

Comte provides concrete examples of such laws arising from physical organization and needs:
- Abstaining from food causes pain (or death if prolonged).
- Exposing organs to fire warms or burns them (depending on proximity).
- Depriving oneself of necessary breathable air causes suffering or death (depending on duration/extent).
- Multiplication of the species results from the union of the sexes.
- Pleasures too often repeated or prolonged weaken the organs.
- Moderate exercise strengthens them.[^3]

He frames these alongside broader influences like hunger, thirst, cold, heat, fear, and security, which affect all men equally once habits and development align.[^4] These are not moral or juristic maxims but empirical necessities akin to physical laws (e.g., a seed germinating in earth or a body falling when unsupported), inescapable without penalty.[^5]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Ch. 5 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/05-des-lois-auxquelles-les-hommes-sont-assujettis-par/translation/#p-58c9d4).
[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Ch. 5 [passage](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/05-des-lois-auxquelles-les-hommes-sont-assujettis-par/translation/#p-58c9d4-p-3d7e8a).
[^3]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Ch. 5 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/05-des-lois-auxquelles-les-hommes-sont-assujettis-par/translation/#p-2c4dfb).
[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Ch. 5 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/05-des-lois-auxquelles-les-hommes-sont-assujettis-par/translation/#p-f10f47).
[^5]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Ch. 5 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/05-des-lois-auxquelles-les-hommes-sont-assujettis-par/translation/#p-3d7e8a).
